title: Sanity: Descent Into Madness
description: Brings the sanity mechanic from Don't Starve into Minecraft
body:
![Sanity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/croissantnova/SanityDescentIntoMadness/main/readme/sanitydim128.png) # Sanity: Descent Into Madness A Minecraft mod that brings the sanity mechanic from [Don't Starve](https://store.steampowered.com/app/219740/Dont_Starve/) into Minecraft. ## Requirements

**When updating, make sure to delete the config file so it's recreated and new config changes are applied. You would have to backup your own changes though.** ## Description This mod adds a new vital for you to manage - sanity, your mental health scale. Failing to keep your sanity at high levels may have drastic consequences on your survival, including fatal outcome.

As your sanity drops, you start hearing and seeing things, your vision becomes muddy and blurry, as well as your mind cloudy, until eventually you start getting attacked by the creations of your own mind, inner entities.
Certain mobs will also react accordingly to your unstable mental condition... ![Sanity GIF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/croissantnova/SanityDescentIntoMadness/main/readme/sanity_animation.gif) There are multiple ways to restore your sanity, as well as multiple ways to lose it. Visit the [wiki](https://github.com/croissantnova/SanityDescentIntoMadness/wiki/Activities-that-affect-sanity) to see them all.
The first thing you might want to do, however, is to craft a garland from any small flowers you encounter on your journey. This nice little accessory should help you stay sane for a bit longer. Be wary, though, that water makes it wither away much faster!

## Details The mod is highly configurable, see [configuration file](https://github.com/croissantnova/SanityDescentIntoMadness/blob/main/config.toml).
The mod also supports dimension-specific configuration in ```config/sanitydim/dimension/custom_config.toml```.
You're free to include this mod in your modpacks. ## Known bugs - mc1.16: currently there's a bug with entities' animations, but it shouldn't affect gameplay much ## Credits croissantnova - programmer and artist,
[toujourspareil](https://twitter.com/toujourspareil_) - artist.

Sound effects provided by [Zapsplat](https://www.zapsplat.com/) ## License See [license](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/croissantnova/SanityDescentIntoMadness/main/LICENSE).
